find:
## Falsifiability

### Numeric thresholds (consolidated)
- **Primary endpoint:** PAM-13 Δ ≥ 5.4 points (MCID)
- **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni-adjusted α = 0.025 for each of ≤2 secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.
- **Power:** 0.80
- **Sample size:** N ≥ 55 per group (see Sample size calculation)
- **Consistency note:** All falsifiability thresholds are identical across CONCEPT.md and THEORY.md. Any future updates must be synchronised across both files.
- **Stopping rule:** Adverse event rate >5% in intervention arm (any AE, assessed monthly by DSMB)
- **Interim analysis:** Planned at N=100 (50 per group) for futility only (O'Brien-Fleming boundary, α-spending = 0.005). If conditional power <20%, trial will be stopped for futility.
- **Primary analysis:** Intention-to-treat (ITT); per-protocol sensitivity analysis as secondary
- **Consistency note:** All falsifiability thresholds are identical across CONCEPT.md and THEORY.md. Any future updates must be synchronised across both files.


## Falsifiability

- **Primary endpoint:** PAM-13 Δ ≥ 5.4 points (MCID)
- **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni-adjusted α = 0.025 for each of ≤2 secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.
- **Note:** This section consolidates all falsifiability criteria. Duplicate sections have been removed. The α = 0.05 threshold is consistent across all core documents.
- **Power:** 0.80
- **Sample size:** N ≥ 55 per group (see Sample size calculation)
- **Stopping rule:** Adverse event rate >5% in intervention arm (any AE, assessed monthly by DSMB)
- **Interim analysis:** Planned at N=100 (50 per group) for futility only (O'Brien-Fleming boundary, α-spending = 0.005). If conditional power <20%, trial will be stopped for futility.
- **Primary analysis:** Intention-to-treat (ITT); per-protocol sensitivity analysis as secondary

## Falsifiability

### Numeric thresholds (consolidated)
- **Primary endpoint:** PAM-13 Δ ≥ 5.4 points (MCID)
- **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni-adjusted α = 0.025 for each of ≤2 secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.
- **Power:** 0.80
- **Sample size:** N ≥ 55 per group (see Sample size calculation)
- **Stopping rule:** Adverse event rate >5% in intervention arm (any AE, assessed monthly by DSMB)
- **Interim analysis:** Planned at N=100 (50 per group) for futility only (O'Brien-Fleming boundary, α-spending = 0.005). If conditional power <20%, trial will be stopped for futility.
- **Primary analysis:** Intention-to-treat (ITT); per-protocol sensitivity analysis as secondary
- **Consistency note:** All falsifiability thresholds are identical across CONCEPT.md and THEORY.md. Any future updates must be synchronised across both files.

heading: ## Sample size calculation
body:
### Power analysis sketch
- **Formula:** n = (1.96 + 0.84)² · σ² / δ²
- **Parameters:** α = 0.05 (two-sided), power = 0.80, δ = MCID = 5.4 points
- **Standard deviation:** σ = 10 (placeholder; source: PMID TBD — note: previously cited PMID 15527447 is incorrect for Hibbard 2004; correct reference to be updated)
- **Result:** n ≥ 55 per group (rounded up)
- **Note:** Calculation assumes equal allocation, two-sample t-test. Final sample size may be adjusted for attrition (expected 15–20%).

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| Probability | Impact | Risk description | Mitigation |
|------------|--------|------------------|------------|
| Medium | High | Low recruitment rate | Multi-site recruitment, extended enrollment period |
| Low | High | Data breach / privacy violation | Encryption, access controls, IRB-approved data management |
| Medium | Medium | Attrition >20% | Retention strategies (reminders, incentives), sensitivity analysis |
| Low | Medium | Algorithm performance degradation | Regular model retraining, monitoring of PAM-13 prediction accuracy |
| Medium | Low | Staff turnover | Cross-training, detailed SOPs, backup personnel |

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- Single-blind design (participants cannot be blinded to intervention type)
- Reliance on self-reported outcomes (PAM-13, MMAS-8, EQ-5D-5L)
- Potential for selection bias (volunteer sample)
- Limited generalisability to non-English-speaking populations (all materials in English)
- Short follow-up (6 months for primary outcome; 12 months for hospitalisations)
- Placeholder sample size standard deviation (σ = 10) requires validation from published literature
